creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is not what I asked for. Your $POUDRIERE_ETC environment variable points to your poudriere configuration:


cwsys# env | grep POUDRIERE
POUDRIERE_ETC=/var/db/local/poudriere-cwsys
cwsys# ls $POUDRIERE_ETC/poudriere.d
HEADamd64-new-ports-options     make.conf
HEADamd64-options               new-ports-options
hooks                           ports
jails                           prod-options
cwsys# 

bob# ls $POUDRIERE_ETC/poudriere.d
10i386-options                  make.conf
HEADi386-new-ports-options      new-ports-options
HEADi386-options                ports
hooks                           prod-options
jails
bob# 


I need the contents of all the *-options directories files listed to understand what ports your syslog-ng depend on options are. Your curl has a different option set and using your default SSL I get a circular dependency, and therefore cannot reproduce your problem.

On my two poudriere build systems I maintain four different options dirs. I will need a copy of the ftp/curl options from the jail. If not the jail from the ports tree you build agains. (It's a hierarchy.)

Otherwise I don't see how your build even got as far as your sample shows it did (and your problems are more systemic than simply syslog-ng).
